raw_response:
{
  "rephrased_requirement": "Implement a web‑based AI Q&A feature that allows authenticated users to upload documents (PDF, DOCX, TXT, up to 50 MB each), automatically extracts and indexes the content, and then answers natural‑language questions about the uploaded material. The system must enforce data confidentiality, respond to queries within 2 seconds for typical workloads, and achieve a minimum answer‑accuracy of 80 % on a representative benchmark while logging all activity for audit.",
  "functional_requirements": [
    "User authentication and authorization (OAuth2 / OpenID Connect) before any operation.",
    "File upload endpoint supporting PDF, DOCX, TXT with size limit 50 MB per file.",
    "Automatic virus/malware scanning of every uploaded file.",
    "Document preprocessing: text extraction, language detection, chunking (≈ 500‑token chunks).",
    "Embedding generation for each chunk using a configurable LLM embedding model.",
    "Storage of embeddings and metadata in a vector database with per‑user isolation.",
    "Query endpoint that accepts a natural‑language question, retrieves relevant chunks, and generates an answer via a generative LLM.",
    "Answer provenance: return source chunk references (document name, page/section) with each answer.",
    "User‑level activity log (upload, query, download) stored immutable for audit.",
    "Admin dashboard to monitor usage, health, and security alerts."
  ],
  "non_functional_requirements": [
    "Performance: 95 % of queries must return a response within 2 seconds for a workload of up to 100 concurrent users and a total indexed corpus of 10 GB.",
    "Scalability: Horizontal scaling of ingestion and query services to handle up to 1,000 concurrent users without degradation.",
    "Availability: 99.9 % uptime measured monthly, with automated failover across two availability zones.",
    "Reliability: Automatic retry and dead‑letter handling for failed ingestion or inference jobs.",
    "Observability: Metrics (latency, error rate, CPU/memory), logs, and distributed tracing exported to Prometheus/Grafana.",
    "Data retention: Uploaded documents and embeddings are retained for a configurable period (default 30 days) and then securely deleted.",
    "Compliance: GDPR‑compatible data handling, with ability to export or erase a user’s data on request."
  ],
  "security_requirements": [
    "All network traffic must be encrypted with TLS 1.2+.",
    "At‑rest encryption for stored files, embeddings, and logs using AES‑256.",
    "Role‑based access control (RBAC): only owners and admins can view or delete a document.",
    "Isolation: each tenant’s data stored in separate logical namespace within the vector store.",
    "Virus scanning using a up‑to‑date AV engine (e.g., ClamAV) before ingestion.",
    "Audit logging of every upload, download, and query with user ID, timestamp, and IP address.",
    "Rate limiting: max 20 queries per minute per user to mitigate abuse.",
    "Secret management: API keys, model credentials, and encryption keys stored in a vault (e.g., HashiCorp Vault or cloud KMS).",
    "Regular security testing: quarterly penetration test and continuous static code analysis."
  ],
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "A registered user can successfully upload a 45 MB PDF and receive a 200 OK response within 5 seconds.",
    "Uploaded files are scanned; a malicious file is rejected with a clear error message.",
    "After ingestion, a user asks a factual question about the document and receives an answer in ≤2 seconds, with source citations displayed.",
    "On a benchmark dataset (e.g., SQuAD‑derived document set), the system achieves ≥80 % exact‑match accuracy (or F1 ≥0.80).",
    "All data in transit is captured by Wireshark as TLS‑encrypted; no plaintext is observed.",
    "Data at rest is verified encrypted (e.g., using cloud provider’s encryption‑at‑rest verification tools).",
    "Audit logs contain correct user ID, operation type, timestamp, and IP for every action.",
    "System maintains ≥99.9 % availability over a 30‑day observation period.",
    "When a user requests data deletion, all associated files, embeddings, and logs are removed within 24 hours."
  ],
  "recommended_solution_approach": "Adopt a micro‑service architecture deployed on a Kubernetes cluster. Key components: • API Gateway (e.g., Kong or Envoy) for routing, TLS termination, and rate limiting. • Auth Service (Keycloak) handling OAuth2/OIDC. • Ingestion Service (Python FastAPI) that receives files, runs ClamAV, extracts text (pdfminer, docx2txt), chunks, and calls an embedding model (OpenAI embeddings or a self‑hosted Sentence‑Transformer). • Vector Store (Pinecone, Qdrant, or Milvus) with per‑tenant namespaces. • Retrieval‑Augmented Generation Service that queries the vector store, assembles context, and calls a generative LLM (OpenAI GPT‑4o or an on‑prem Llama‑2 via vLLM). • Audit & Logging Service (ELK stack). • Monitoring stack (Prometheus + Grafana). Use Helm charts for reproducible deployments, CI/CD pipelines for automated testing, and IaC (Terraform) for cloud resources. Security is enforced at every layer: mTLS between services, secret injection from Vault, and regular vulnerability scanning."
  ,
  "open_questions": [
    "What is the maximum expected concurrent user count and peak query rate?",
    "Which document formats beyond PDF/DOCX/TXT are required (e.g., images, spreadsheets)?",
    "Is there a preferred LLM provider (OpenAI, Azure, self‑hosted) and any cost constraints?",
    "What specific accuracy metric is most important (exact match, F1, BLEU, human rating)?",
    "What is the required data retention period and any legal hold requirements?",
    "Will the system be multi‑tenant (multiple organizations) or single‑tenant per customer?",
    "Are there existing identity providers to integrate with (Azure AD, Okta, etc.)?",
    "What is the budget for third‑party services such as vector DB or LLM API usage?",
    "Do we need offline/on‑prem deployment options for highly regulated environments?"
  ]
}

parsed_output.rephrased_requirement: Implement a web‑based AI Q&A feature that allows authenticated users to upload documents (PDF, DOCX, TXT, up to 50 MB each), automatically extracts and indexes the content, and then answers natural‑language questions about the uploaded material. The system must enforce data confidentiality, respond to queries within 2 seconds for typical workloads, and achieve a minimum answer‑accuracy of 80 % on a representative benchmark while logging all activity for audit.
functional_requirements: User authentication and authorization (OAuth2 / OpenID Connect) before any operation.
File upload endpoint supporting PDF, DOCX, TXT with size limit 50 MB per file.
Automatic virus/malware scanning of every uploaded file.
Document preprocessing: text extraction, language detection, chunking (≈ 500‑token chunks).
Embedding generation for each chunk using a configurable LLM embedding model.
Storage of embeddings and metadata in a vector database with per‑user isolation.
Query endpoint that accepts a natural‑language question, retrieves relevant chunks, and generates an answer via a generative LLM.
Answer provenance: return source chunk references (document name, page/section) with each answer.
User‑level activity log (upload, query, download) stored immutable for audit.
Admin dashboard to monitor usage, health, and security alerts.
non_functional_requirements: Performance: 95 % of queries must return a response within 2 seconds for a workload of up to 100 concurrent users and a total indexed corpus of 10 GB.
Scalability: Horizontal scaling of ingestion and query services to handle up to 1,000 concurrent users without degradation.
Availability: 99.9 % uptime measured monthly, with automated failover across two availability zones.
Reliability: Automatic retry and dead‑letter handling for failed ingestion or inference jobs.
Observability: Metrics (latency, error rate, CPU/memory), logs, and distributed tracing exported to Prometheus/Grafana.
Data retention: Uploaded documents and embeddings are retained for a configurable period (default 30 days) and then securely deleted.
Compliance: GDPR‑compatible data handling, with ability to export or erase a user’s data on request.
security_requirements: All network traffic must be encrypted with TLS 1.2+.
At‑rest encryption for stored files, embeddings, and logs using AES‑256.
Role‑based access control (RBAC): only owners and admins can view or delete a document.
Isolation: each tenant’s data stored in separate logical namespace within the vector store.
Virus scanning using a up‑to‑date AV engine (e.g., ClamAV) before ingestion.
Audit logging of every upload, download, and query with user ID, timestamp, and IP address.
Rate limiting: max 20 queries per minute per user to mitigate abuse.
Secret management: API keys, model credentials, and encryption keys stored in a vault (e.g., HashiCorp Vault or cloud KMS).
Regular security testing: quarterly penetration test and continuous static code analysis.
acceptance_criteria: A registered user can successfully upload a 45 MB PDF and receive a 200 OK response within 5 seconds.
Uploaded files are scanned; a malicious file is rejected with a clear error message.
After ingestion, a user asks a factual question about the document and receives an answer in ≤2 seconds, with source citations displayed.
On a benchmark dataset (e.g., SQuAD‑derived document set), the system achieves ≥80 % exact‑match accuracy (or F1 ≥0.80).
All data in transit is captured by Wireshark as TLS‑encrypted; no plaintext is observed.
Data at rest is verified encrypted (e.g., using cloud provider’s encryption‑at‑rest verification tools).
Audit logs contain correct user ID, operation type, timestamp, and IP for every action.
System maintains ≥99.9 % availability over a 30‑day observation period.
When a user requests data deletion, all associated files, embeddings, and logs are removed within 24 hours.
parsed_output.recommended_solution_approach: Adopt a micro‑service architecture deployed on a Kubernetes cluster. Key components: • API Gateway (e.g., Kong or Envoy) for routing, TLS termination, and rate limiting. • Auth Service (Keycloak) handling OAuth2/OIDC. • Ingestion Service (Python FastAPI) that receives files, runs ClamAV, extracts text (pdfminer, docx2txt), chunks, and calls an embedding model (OpenAI embeddings or a self‑hosted Sentence‑Transformer). • Vector Store (Pinecone, Qdrant, or Milvus) with per‑tenant namespaces. • Retrieval‑Augmented Generation Service that queries the vector store, assembles context, and calls a generative LLM (OpenAI GPT‑4o or an on‑prem Llama‑2 via vLLM). • Audit & Logging Service (ELK stack). • Monitoring stack (Prometheus + Grafana). Use Helm charts for reproducible deployments, CI/CD pipelines for automated testing, and IaC (Terraform) for cloud resources. Security is enforced at every layer: mTLS between services, secret injection from Vault, and regular vulnerability scanning.
open_questions: What is the maximum expected concurrent user count and peak query rate?
Which document formats beyond PDF/DOCX/TXT are required (e.g., images, spreadsheets)?
Is there a preferred LLM provider (OpenAI, Azure, self‑hosted) and any cost constraints?
What specific accuracy metric is most important (exact match, F1, BLEU, human rating)?
What is the required data retention period and any legal hold requirements?
Will the system be multi‑tenant (multiple organizations) or single‑tenant per customer?
Are there existing identity providers to integrate with (Azure AD, Okta, etc.)?
